title: tecticornia willisii
intro: Tecticornia willisii, also known as the saltbush or beach glasswort, is a succulent shrub native to the coastal regions of Western Australia. As a medicinal plant, Tecticornia willisii has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. The herbal preparations of Tecticornia willisii typically involve drying and powdering the leaves, which can then be used in teas, salves, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
